content.text: Parallel Eval and Test Pipeline Design
A naive CI/CD pipeline runs stages sequentially: lint, then unit tests, then evals. For a Claude application with hundreds of eval cases and a probe library, this adds
to every PR. Parallel pipeline design fans out independent jobs so they run concurrently, cutting wall-clock time to the duration of the slowest single job.
The goal is not to run fewer checks — it is to run all checks in parallel so the full safety envelope is enforced without slowing engineers down.

This sub-subtopic covers how to structure a parallel pipeline, handle the fan-in barrier correctly, partition eval suites for efficient batching, manage API concurrency limits, and integrate the result into merge gates.

attrs.alt: Parallel eval and test pipeline design with fan-out, fan-in, and staging deploy

content.text: The five gate types in a Claude CI pipeline are mutually independent at the PR stage: lint has no dependency on evals, and contract tests have no dependency on security probes. Running them in parallel reduces total CI time from the sum of all stages to the maximum of any single stage:
In GitHub Actions, parallel jobs are defined as separate entries under jobs: with no needs: dependency between them. Each starts immediately when the workflow triggers.

# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  # ── Fan-out: all five jobs start simultaneously ──────────────────────────

  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install ruff mypy
      - run: ruff check . && mypy src/ --strict

  unit-tests:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
      - run: pytest tests/unit/ -x --tb=short

  contract-tests:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
      - run: pytest tests/contract/ --tb=short

  eval-gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install anthropic pytest
      - name: Run eval gate
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
        run: pytest tests/evals/ --eval-threshold=0.85 --tb=short

  security-gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install anthropic pytest
      - name: Run probe replay
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
        run: pytest tests/red_team/test_probe_replay.py -v --tb=short

  # ── Fan-in barrier: staging only runs after ALL five pass ────────────────

  staging-deploy:
    needs: [lint, unit-tests, contract-tests, eval-gate, security-gate]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Deploy to staging
        run: ./scripts/deploy.sh staging
      - name: Run E2E tests
        env:
          STAGING_APP_URL: ${{ secrets.STAGING_APP_URL }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
        run: pytest tests/e2e/ --tb=short
The eval gate is typically the slowest job. If your eval suite has more than 50 cases, shard it across multiple runners so the cases run in parallel rather than serially:

# .github/workflows/ci.yml (eval-gate with matrix sharding)
  eval-gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        shard: [1, 2, 3, 4]    # 4 runners process the suite in quarters
      fail-fast: true           # cancel remaining shards if one fails
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }
      - run: pip install anthropic pytest
      - name: Run eval shard ${{ matrix.shard }} of 4
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          pytest tests/evals/             --eval-threshold=0.85             --shard=${{ matrix.shard }}             --total-shards=4             --tb=short

# tests/evals/conftest.py (shard-aware collection)
import pytest

_results = []

def pytest_addoption(parser):
    parser.addoption("--eval-threshold", default="0.85")
    parser.addoption("--shard", default="1")
    parser.addoption("--total-shards", default="1")

def pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items):
    """Assign each test to exactly one shard using modulo partitioning."""
    shard = int(config.getoption("--shard"))
    total = int(config.getoption("--total-shards"))
    if total <= 1:
        return
    # Keep items whose index belongs to this shard
    selected = [item for i, item in enumerate(items) if (i % total) + 1 == shard]
    items[:] = selected

def pytest_runtest_logreport(report):
    if report.when == "call":
        _results.append(report.passed)

def pytest_sessionfinish(session, exitstatus):
    threshold = float(session.config.getoption("--eval-threshold"))
    if not _results:
        return
    pass_rate = sum(_results) / len(_results)
    print(f"\nShard pass rate: {pass_rate:.1%} (threshold: {threshold:.1%})")
    if pass_rate < threshold:
        session.exitstatus = 1
Parallel jobs all share the same API key. Without rate-limit awareness, multiple shards can saturate the per-minute token budget and cause 429 errors that appear as test failures.

# tests/evals/api_client.py
"""Rate-limit-aware eval client for use in parallel CI shards."""
import time, threading, anthropic
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any

@dataclass
class RateLimitedClient:
    """Wraps Anthropic client with a simple token-bucket for CI eval runs."""
    requests_per_minute: int = 50    # conservative; raise if tier allows
    _lock: threading.Lock = field(default_factory=threading.Lock, repr=False)
    _timestamps: list = field(default_factory=list, repr=False)
    _client: Any = field(default_factory=anthropic.Anthropic, repr=False)

    def create(self, **kwargs):
        """Call messages.create with per-minute rate limiting."""
        with self._lock:
            now = time.monotonic()
            # Drop timestamps older than 60 s
            self._timestamps = [t for t in self._timestamps if now - t < 60]
            if len(self._timestamps) >= self.requests_per_minute:
                sleep_s = 60 - (now - self._timestamps[0]) + 0.1
                time.sleep(max(0, sleep_s))
            self._timestamps.append(time.monotonic())
        return self._client.messages.create(**kwargs)

# Use this in eval fixtures instead of the raw client
_eval_client = RateLimitedClient()

def eval_create(**kwargs):
    return _eval_client.create(**kwargs)
Even with rate limiting, four shards starting simultaneously create a burst. Add a small stagger using the shard index:
Each parallel job installs Python dependencies from scratch by default. Cache the pip install step so runners reuse the site-packages directory:

# Reusable step fragment (add to every job)
      - name: Cache pip
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles("**/requirements*.txt") }}
          restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
For larger projects, consider a shared Docker image with dependencies pre-baked to eliminate install time entirely.
When shards run in parallel, test results are scattered across multiple runner logs. Collect them into a single report by uploading JUnit XML from each shard and merging them in the fan-in job:

# .github/workflows/ci.yml (eval-gate shard: upload results)
      - name: Upload shard results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: eval-results-shard-${{ matrix.shard }}
          path: reports/eval_results_shard_${{ matrix.shard }}.xml

  # Fan-in: download and merge
  eval-report:
    needs: [eval-gate]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: always()
    steps:
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          pattern: eval-results-shard-*
          merge-multiple: true
          path: reports/
      - name: Merge JUnit reports
        run: |
          pip install junitparser
          python - <<'EOF'
          from junitparser import JUnitXml
          import pathlib
          merged = JUnitXml()
          for f in pathlib.Path("reports").glob("*.xml"):
              merged += JUnitXml.fromfile(str(f))
          merged.write("reports/eval_combined.xml")
          print(f"Total tests: {merged.tests}, failures: {merged.failures}")
          EOF
      - name: Publish test summary
        uses: EnricoMi/publish-unit-test-result-action@v2
        with:
          files: reports/eval_combined.xml
Not every push needs the full eval suite. Use conditional execution to skip expensive API-calling stages for obvious non-changes:

# Only run eval-gate and security-gate when relevant files change
  eval-gate:
    if: |
      github.event_name == 'push' ||
      contains(github.event.pull_request.changed_files, 'prompts/') ||
      contains(github.event.pull_request.changed_files, 'src/') ||
      contains(github.event.pull_request.changed_files, 'tests/evals/')
    # ... rest of job
Fan out all independent gate jobs (lint, unit, contract, eval, security) in parallel; fan them back in with a needs: barrier before staging deploy
Shard large eval suites across multiple runners using modulo partitioning; keep per-shard time under 5 minutes to match the slowest non-eval job
Use a rate-limited eval client and stagger shard start times to avoid 429 bursts from concurrent runners sharing one API key
Cache pip dependencies and consider a pre-baked Docker image to eliminate per-job install overhead
Upload JUnit XML from each shard and merge in a downstream fan-in job so reports span the full suite
Conditionally skip API-calling stages for changes that cannot affect model behaviour (docs-only, probe file only, etc.)
The all-pass barrier (needs: all five jobs) is mandatory — the staging deploy and merge gate must not run if any parallel job fails
